opp.name: McCain_S2P_2025
opp.next_step: 6/16 - Amanda Moir who reports directly to new CPO Helen Whitehouse is scheduled to attend June 25th Toronto event. Have EM'd and WhatsApp'd Ludovic with no response to date. Inviting Amanda and Helen to Horizon in Sept. 4/25MF - no response to our reach outs. Invited a number of McCain procurement titles to come to our booth at Gartner May 4-6 in Orlando. Working to get a live call set with Hackett Group and inviting to Horizon in September. 03/10mc- McCain remains active, but timing is being shaped by recent organizational restructuring and leadership transition. Spoke on the phone with the new CPO, who asked that we reconnect at the end of March. Sending invite for March 30th to reconnect with her. Ludovic has been out of office and is expected back by end of this week, at which point we will confirm continuity, revalidate what remains in scope from the analytics initiative, and assess how much sponsorship carries forward. Strategy is to keep both threads warm and aligned so we preserve the work already done with Ludovic while creating an opening with the new CPO; key risk is disruption or reprioritization from the internal changes.
opp.ais_why:
• Champion Ludovic Verdelet RSVP'd to the Vienna Zycus AI procurement event and asked about Heineken's AI-focused session, including how AI shows up in iNegotiate and iContract. That's curiosity about an AI-themed event, not a buyer-stated need for AI in the McCain deal.
• The most recent 2/13/2026 Avoma call was housekeeping and event coordination, with no Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA, or Intake mentioned by McCain. Earlier discovery shows Merlin and Agentic AI in scope and a mid-2025 POC on taxonomy and automation, but that work paused when the CPO transitioned and AI dialogue has gone quiet since.
• Ludovic also flagged that the team is still waiting for clarity on McCain's coming reorganization, with Ellen Whitehouse newly in the CPO seat and no roadmap shared. There is no champion bandwidth for an AI POC right now, and the only recent engagement was event logistics, which keeps this at the cautious end of AI Curious.

items.quote: Zycus has had no direct working conversation with CPO Helen Whitehouse; approval owner unengaged
no cost, savings or cycle-time figure articulated in any call or field
NDA agreed 24 Oct 2025 to be shared with McCain legal; no evidence it was signed

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI has been put to the buyer. The only value signal is qualitative urgency from Ludovic Verdelet on 10 Dec 2025, that 'every month left behind us without moving is a month lost' and that lost time 'is something we'll have to recover.' No cost, savings, cycle-time, headcount or error-rate figure has been attached to the McCain analytics initiative in any call or field. Quantifying the cost of the 8-month delay and the savings a scoped iAnalyze/Intake POC would unlock is the move that gives the incoming CPO a reason to prioritize this over inertia.

champion.narrative: Ludovic Verdelet is the sole champion, genuinely engaged but personally at risk. He has carried the relationship across every recorded call, advocated internally ('our proposal to Helene, let's jump on the POC, we still believe this is something where we have to go', 24 Oct 2025), agreed to the NDA and a late-January enhancements preview, and transparently invited Zycus into the reorg. The risk is twofold: he is single-threaded (no other buyer contact role exists on this opp) and his own future is uncertain ('will I decide to be part of it or not?', 13 Feb 2026), with VP Maxim withholding org detail from him. He has also gone non-responsive to recent email and WhatsApp (Next Step, 16 Jun 2026). Amanda Moir's 25 Jun event engagement is the first sign of a second thread; developing her is now urgent.

sources: Avoma call 10 Dec 2025 (Ludovic Verdelet, Alexa Bradley)
competition.narrative: The dominant competitor is Do Nothing / organizational inertia: the project is frozen behind the McCain reorg, not behind a rival vendor, and on 24 Oct 2025 Ludovic himself chose to pause. He acknowledged on 10 Dec 2025 that McCain must understand 'the different offers on the market and the functionalities of each tool,' and Alexa Bradley noted the same day 'it may not be just us, it might be 6 other suppliers,' so a multi-vendor field is anticipated but unnamed in current evidence. Coupa, SpendHQ and an in-house/manual approach are carried on record from prior sweeps with no fresh mention this run, so they hold as historical context, not fresh threats. The wedge is the Merlin/Agentic AI and analytics story (intake, tail spend, ANA) that Amanda Moir responded to on 25 Jun, used to differentiate before a formal bake-off forms.

sources: deal record (original POC scope: taxonomy accuracy + automation)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is real but qualitative. McCain wants to advance an approved analytics strategy and modernize upstream procurement (intake, tail/tactical spend, taxonomy accuracy and automation per the POC scope), and Ludovic repeatedly framed the delay itself as pain, 'every month left behind us without moving is a month lost' (10 Dec 2025). The deeper confirmed pain is organizational disruption blocking a strategy Ludovic personally champions; the pain owner is procurement (Ludovic, the incoming CPO and the forming COE). The gap is that the operational or financial cost of today's manual/in-house approach has never been articulated in numbers.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics begin and end at an unexecuted NDA. On 24 Oct 2025 Ludovic agreed to move on the NDA immediately ('it makes sense for me to go on the NDA right now, this is just a way to protect the company') and committed to share the Zycus template with McCain legal after his PTO. No later call or Next Step evidences the NDA being signed, and no MSA/SOW, procurement channel, InfoSec review, or partner/SI scoping has surfaced. Until the NDA is executed, POC data-sharing and commercials stay blocked; confirming NDA status is the cheapest unblock and would create visibility into McCain's paper process.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is identified by name and title but not engaged. Helen Whitehouse (Chief Procurement Officer, an internal promotion from the relevant stakeholder) is the new CPO who must re-approve the analytics strategy before the POC restarts, per Ludovic on 24 Oct 2025: 'I'd rather have this strategy approved by Ellen right now because she will be the one for the years to come.' Salesforce Next Step (16 Jun 2026) confirms she owns approval and that Zycus is trying to reach her through a Sept Horizon invite. She has never had a direct working conversation with Zycus, so commercial appetite and view of the analytics strategy are unknown; securing a direct COE alignment session is the gating move.

decision_process.narrative: The process is clear in shape but stalled on approval. Ludovic confirmed on 24 Oct 2025 this stays 'procurement ownership, procurement teams, procurement management, and general management if needed,' with a newly forming procurement Center of Excellence (COE) to own analytics (10 Dec 2025). The gate is the incoming Helen Whitehouse re-approving the analytics strategy after she finalizes the reorg, targeted mid/end Feb 2026 but still unresolved. Ludovic's VP Maxim is withholding org detail even from Ludovic (13 Feb 2026), so the approval chain above procurement is opaque. The next step in the buyer's own process is CPO sign-off, which unblocks the POC.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are partly known from the original scope and Ludovic's stated intent, not from a formal RFP round. The POC was scoped on taxonomy accuracy and automation (upstream S2C, intake, tail/tactical spend), and on 10 Dec 2025 Ludovic said understanding 'the different offers on the market and the functionalities of each tool will help us go fast,' signalling a functional comparison will drive selection. Amanda Moir's 25 Jun 2026 interest in Agentic AI use cases for Intake/Orchestration and ANA adds an AI-impact criterion. The formal, buyer-documented evaluation criteria have not yet been captured because the strategy is being re-approved under the new CPO.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. It's genuinely competitive and could go either way; the reads below say where it stands.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 55.
blocks.read: We're in it, with a slight edge.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Ludovic Verdelet is a genuine, engaged advocate who has carried the relationship and pushed the POC internally, but he is single-threaded, personally at risk in the reorg, and has recently gone quiet on rep outreach. Amanda Moir is an emerging second thread but not yet a champion.
bullets.text: Champion is driving it — Ludovic Verdelet is a genuine, engaged advocate who has carried the relationship and pushed the POC internally, but he is single-threaded, personally…

blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.
blocks.title: Deal momentum

bullets.text: Live next steps with named dates

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: The strongest current threat is Do Nothing / organizational inertia: an ~8-month internal reorg has frozen the deal and the buyer paused it himself on 24 Oct 2025.

bullets.text: Economic buyer (Helen Whitehouse) has never been directly engaged and owns the approval that unblocks the POC.
POC on hold about 8 months; every restart target (Nov 2025, Jan 2026, Feb 2026, Mar 2026) has slipped with no forcing function.
Single-threaded on Ludovic, whose own role is uncertain in the reorg and who has gone non-responsive to recent outreach.

items: Book a working session with Amanda Moir on Intake/Orchestration and ANA quick wins. (by 10 Jul)
Reach Ludovic through a fresh channel or via Amanda to confirm CPO approval status. (by 17 Jul)
Secure a direct alignment session with Helen Whitehouse or the procurement COE. (by 24 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: McCain Foods Limited — Shortlisted · $150K · closes 26 Feb

contributions.evidence: Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 55 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 52 vs stage-expected 56.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 4 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 31 is anchored on the stage win (55), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 32. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 4 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

confidence_signals.summary: Mixed: champion cooling and non-responsive, but a fresh second thread (Amanda Moir) engaged well on Agentic AI at the 25 Jun event.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 1 Aug 2025, about 11 months old. In Shortlisted with close 26 Feb 2027 (238 days out). Last Salesforce activity 26 Jun 2026 is rep-logged from the Toronto event; last recorded buyer CALL was 13 Feb 2026 (about 4.7 months ago). The POC has sat on hold since 24 Oct 2025 and missed every restart target (Nov 2025 CPO approval, Jan 2026 POC start, Feb 2026 org clarity, Mar 2026 CPO reconnect). A Feb 2027 close is still reachable if the incoming CPO unblocks soon, but there is no forcing function and the champion has gone quiet.
evidence: POC placed on hold pending incoming CPO approval (Ludovic Verdelet, 24 Oct 2025 call): 'I proposed and it has been accepted to put things on hold. I don't want you, me, and our teams to invest time in something that should deliver nothing.'
Org still undefined as of 13 Feb 2026 (Ludovic Verdelet): 'I don't know what will be the organization, who will be in, who will be out' and 'will I decide to be part of it or not?'
SF Next_Step__c (16 Jun 2026): rep has emailed and WhatsApp'd Ludovic 'with no response to date.'

SF Task (26 Jun 2026): 'Amanda Moir attended Toronto RT event and had good questions around AI deployment and AgenticAI use cases, supporting Intake/Orchestration and ANA as 2 areas which can drive immediate impact.'
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal frozen at the CPO-approval gate for about 8 months; Ludovic non-responsive to recent outreach, but Amanda Moir engaged well on Agentic AI at the 25 Jun Toronto event, opening a fresh thread.

flags: Multi-thread now: convert Amanda Moir's 25 Jun Agentic AI interest into a working contact, since Ludovic is single-threaded and personally at risk in the reorg.
Turn the CPO transition into a forcing function: secure a direct alignment session with Helen Whitehouse/the COE to re-approve the analytics strategy with a dated cost-of-delay case.
Lead with the Merlin/Agentic AI Intake and ANA story that Amanda responded to, differentiating Zycus before an anticipated multi-vendor bake-off forms.
best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for a single-threaded, reorg-frozen Shortlisted deal: build a second thread, convert the new CPO into a forcing function, and lead with the AI narrative that just landed.

customer_preference.evidence: Amanda Moir asked strong Agentic AI use-case questions (Intake/Orchestration, ANA) at the 25 Jun event and Ludovic wants to see Zycus enhancements, but no decisive Zycus-over-rival lean stated.

competitive_position.summary: The strongest current threat is Do Nothing / organizational inertia: an ~8-month internal reorg has frozen the deal and the buyer paused it himself on 24 Oct 2025. A formal multi-vendor evaluation is anticipated ('it might be 6 other suppliers', Alexa Bradley, 10 Dec 2025) but no rival is named in current evidence. Coupa, SpendHQ and an in-house/manual approach remain on record from prior sweeps with no fresh mention this run, carried as lower-priority historical context.

• Champion Ludovic Verdelet RSVP'd to the Vienna Zycus AI procurement event and asked about Heineken's AI-focused session, including how AI shows up in iNegotiate and iContract. That's curiosity about an AI-themed event, not a buyer-stated need for AI in the McCain deal.
• The most recent 2/13/2026 Avoma call was housekeeping and event coordination, with no Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA, or Intake mentioned by McCain. Earlier discovery shows Merlin and Agentic AI in scope and a mid-2025 POC on taxonomy and automation, but that work paused when the CPO transitioned and AI dialogue has gone quiet since.
• Ludovic also flagged that the team is still waiting for clarity on McCain's coming reorganization, with Ellen Whitehouse newly in the CPO seat and no roadmap shared. There is no champion bandwidth for an AI POC right now, and the only recent engagement was event logistics, which keeps this at the cautious end of AI Curious.

Ludovic has been out of office and is expected back by end of this week, at which point we will confirm continuity, revalidate what remains in scope from the analytics initiative, and assess how much sponsorship carries forward.
